title: Kernel bootstrap for this Codex session. summary_text: **Findings (severity-ordered)** - `Critical` Degraded mode can be accepted without proving “2 GLM failures in same run” if receipt is written as `degraded-allowed`. Health/phase logic trusts `receipt_mode == degraded-allowed` OR `glm_mode == degraded-allowed`, but only the `glm_mode` path is tied to failure threshold. Evidence: [kernel-runtime-health.sh:200](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh), [kernel-phase-gate.sh:222](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-phase-gate.sh), [kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh:49](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh), policy requirement in [kernel.md:34](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md). - `High` GLM-unavailable-at-bootstrap does not auto-switch to degraded shape; launch instead fails on missing GLM credentials unless mode is already degraded. This conflicts with prompt contract. Evidence: [codex-kernel-guard.sh:513](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh), [codex-kernel-guard.sh:486](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh), required behavior in [kernel.md:35](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md). - `High` Bootstrap specialist selection is fixed-order command-presence, not dynamic health/quota readiness. It does not use specialist picker scoring/readiness. Evidence: [codex-kernel-guard.sh:414](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh), [codex-kernel-guard.sh:374](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh), dynamic picker exists in [kernel-specialist-picker.sh:278](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-specialist-picker.sh), policy in [kernel.md:30](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md). - `Medium` Lane/manifest evidence is mostly self-reported via env-driven receipt fields; health validates those fields but does not verify real 6 active lanes/processes. Evidence: [kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh:54](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh), [kernel-runtime-health.sh:179](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh), [kernel-runtime-health.sh:185](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh). - `Medium` Mode drift risk: `glm-fail` updates GLM state and ledger but does not rewrite bootstrap receipt, while prompt requires receipt update on mode changes. Evidence: [codex-kernel-guard.sh:748](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh), requirement in [kernel.md:99](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md). - `Low` Launch pre-records `codex success` before actual lane work, which can overstate provider evidence early. Evidence: [codex-kernel-guard.sh:533](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh). **Guardrails for new implementation** - Enforce degraded transition from GLM state, not receipt declaration: gate degraded acceptance on `glm_failures >= threshold` in [kernel-glm-run-state.sh:109](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-glm-run-state.sh), then allow receipt/phase health. - Make bootstrap specialist choice go through readiness+quota path (`pick`/`ready`) from [kernel-specialist-picker.sh:278](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-specialist-picker.sh) instead of fixed-order `specialist_available`. - On any mode change, atomically update both GLM state and bootstrap receipt (`mode`, `providers`, `active_models`, manifest label flags) to avoid health drift. - Keep fail-closed checks in launch/health as invariant gates (`lane_count>=6`, `active_model_count>=3`, manifest labels, provider success evidence) from [kernel-runtime-health.sh:179...
